div.h1: io_uring vs epoll: Shared Memory Ring Buffers

div.p: Performance Impact: With 10K I/O ops/sec, epoll costs ~10-20ms in syscalls. io_uring: ~0.5-1ms. Key Insight: The shared memory ring eliminates the kernel boundary crossing for every operation.

g.text: Ring = Circular buffer in shared memory
Zero-copy = Data stays in original buffer

div.p: Per-operation overhead: • Context switch: ~500-1000 ns • Data copy: user ↔ kernel • 3+ syscalls per I/O cycle
Batched operations: • 1 syscall for N operations • Zero-copy data path • Kernel & app share memory

g.text: Syscalls per I/O | epoll: 2-3 per op | io_uring: 1 per batch
Data path | epoll: Copy user↔kernel | io_uring: Zero-copy (registered)
Notification | epoll: epoll_wait blocks | io_uring: Poll shared memory
Batching | epoll: Not supported | io_uring: Native batch submit
File I/O | epoll: Blocking only | io_uring: True async

div.p: Event Notification "FD 3 is readable" "FD 5 is writable"
Async Processing: • Reads SQ entries • Executes I/O in parallel • Writes results to CQ
